values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

(1 inline comment)

Lets keep this patch about rhel6.5 support.  I'm all for cleanup of older series files, but that should be in a separate commit.

Patch Set 3: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Thanks for expanding the comments!

But I would still like to see cleanup moved to a separate commit from the rhel6.5 support.  Adding rhel6.5 support should not result in changes the rhel6.4, rhel6.3, etc. patch sets.

Patch Set 3:

Hi, Christopher, I don't Think so. Since remove ext4_walk_space will impacted every distro, So we must change rhel6.4, 6.3 patch to adopt this. Else we have to take a temporary way to deal with it. If we haven't doing this work, then you are right. But we already have a patch commit to here. Why don't finish it in one shot?

Patch Set 3:

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.5/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/652c6a10-63bf-11e3-801e-52540035b04c).  Ran 17 tests.  4 tests failed: ost-pools, replay-single, sanityn, sanity.

Patch Set 3: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(5 inline comments)

I'm happy to see some cleanup of this code, if it does not impact performnce.  It reduces complexity in the ldiskfs and osd-ldiskfs code that I'm happy to get rid of, and it may be that newer kernels have APIs that we can use.

I do have a concern about using ext4_get_block() instead of the existing code since this would almost certainly impact performance significantly.  It may be that ext4_get_blocks() could keep the performance, but that needs to be tested first.

Patch Set 3: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

like already said, this basically removes all the improvements we developed to achieve good performance. allocating block at once (even with preallocation) doesn't look very attractive - probably makes sense to look at different method, like  ext4_map_blocks()

Patch Set 15: Code-Review-1

I am kind of wary of moving more of deprecated stuff from ldiskfs into OSD.

I do understand the performance concerns, though we do not have any real numbers for those.

A such let's resurrect this code for rhel6.5 in a patch (I assume all locking remains the same so this is just mechanical resurrection), and also add a configure check (possibly as a separate patch) to detect kernels that don't have this and then use the new way of doing it, then we can measure both ways and if there is a great hit with the new way and if so, decide what to do about it.

Holding that code in osd is just too error prone and we'll need to be on constant lookout for changing locking and such.

Patch Set 18:

This patch has trouble running on real rhel 6.5 kernel (good news is that rhel6.4 operations seems to be unaffected).

I find that when you run racer, it leaks a dentry or file or some such and after racer run you can no longer unmount lustre fs at all (as such, very serious issue).

I also had this crash happen that I have never seen before, while running sanity:

Patch Set 20: Code-Review-1

This patch failed to start in each of the three test runs.

Patch Set 20:

Andreas, failure in tests are due to profile changes in TEI needed for 6.5.  discussed the issue with Minh, he's working on it.  tests should pass after TEI-1168 completes.

Patch Set 20:

Can you explain the problem for those of us not privy to "TEI"?

Patch Set 20:

TEI == Test Engineering and Infrastructure

currently our test framework is setup such that the profiles controlling node provisioning for el6 nodes only do 6.4.  must correct or expand the framework to allow 6.5 as well.

Patch Set 20: Verified-1

no functional change, just rebased to the most current master and without special test-params in the commit header.  now that TEI-1168 is done tests should pass.

parents.subject: LU-4571 fld: resend seq lookup RPC if it is on LWP

commit.subject: LU-4287 kernel: kernel update RHEL6.5 [2.6.32-431.3.1.el6]
commit.message:
LU-4287 kernel: kernel update RHEL6.5 [2.6.32-431.3.1.el6]

Add RHEL6.5 support [2.6.32-431.3.1.el6]

ext4 in RHEL6.5's kernel version 2.6.32-431.3.1.el6 no longer contains
the required function ext4_ext_walk_space(). We start a new rhel6.5
ldiskfs patch series and reintroduce ext4_ext_walk_space() through an
new patch, copying ext4_ext_walk_space() from older kernel rhel6.4
2.6.32-358.23.2.el6.

Signed-off-by: Bob Glossman <bob.glossman@intel.com>
Signed-off-by: yang sheng <yang.sheng@intel.com>
Change-Id: I7112747970343b1264910aa21d7a62c45b5ca1ea
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/8549

Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
